name: a low-confidence OCR-derived health state must not auto-supersede a confident prior
description: docs/ANALYSIS.md 'Guards': low-confidence numeric health facts never auto-supersede anything. Note 1 records a confident medication regimen (lisinopril, conf 0.95). Note 2 is a blurry OCR read the model returns at conf 0.25. Even though the OCR text is surface-attested (so the weight model would commit it), supersession.decide()'s low-confidence guard is keyed on the model's SELF-confidence, not the plan weight: a 0.25 read that would supersede a more-confident active fact is parked as pending_review behind a low_confidence card, and the confident fact stays active. Pins that a blurry OCR value can't silently overwrite a confident fact (regression: integrate must thread self-confidence to decide(), not the plan weight a surface-attested fact carries).

steps.body: Confirmed with Dr. Okafor: I'm on lisinopril 10mg daily.
Photo of the pharmacy label (blurry OCR): looks like it might say losartan 50mg?

facts.statement: Pharmacy label (OCR, low confidence) reads losartan 50mg daily.
